steps.title: Separate coefficients and variable powers
steps.content: Rewrite $(6x^2)(3x^7)$ by grouping the numerical coefficients and the powers of $x$ separately using the associative property: $(6 \cdot 3) \cdot (x^2 \cdot x^7)$.

steps.title: Multiply and apply the exponent rule
steps.content: Multiply the coefficients: $6 \cdot 3 = 18$. For the powers of $x$, apply the product rule for exponents: $x^2 \cdot x^7 = x^{2+7} = x^9$. Combining these results gives $(6x^2)(3x^7) = 18x^9$. Since this is equivalent to $bx^9$, we have $b = 18$.
